original_image_tags: Back of a light-green document folder or file jacket, showing the reverse side with stamps and handwritten notations. The paper is aged with two punch holes at the top, each reinforced with a circular tab (one yellow, one off-white). Text from the reverse side of the folder is faintly visible through the paper (show-through).

elements.description: A scanned document page, likely the back of a typed memo, featuring several rubber stamps and handwritten annotations. Visible elements include a red stamp reading 'ADVISED BY ROUTING', a faded 'FBI' stamp, a purple stamp reading 'RECEIVED-HQUL', a partially obscured red rectangular stamp, and handwritten text including 'Sullivan' and '1/14/44'.

judge.reason: Description A describes a document folder with punch holes and tabs, while Description B quotes specific, contradictory stamp text (e.g., 'ADVISED BY ROUTING', 'RECEIVED-HQUL') and handwritten names not mentioned in A, indicating they are describing different visual content.
